name: ds-team

owner.name: OCHA Centre for Humanitarian Data — Data Science

description: OCHA CHD Data Science team plugins: KB access, data access, data conventions. Style comes from the hdx-ai-hub marketplace (hdx plugin) — not duplicated here. No version fields on purpose: git-SHA versioning means every merged commit auto-deploys.

plugins.name: kb-access
plugins.description: Team knowledge-base access: kb-search + kb-doctor skills, plus a SessionStart hook that clones/updates the local KB (and internal companion) automatically

plugins.name: data-access
plugins.description: Facts for working with team data: blob-io (ocha-stratus blob/Postgres I/O + data semantics) and datasets (third-party humanitarian sources we already have loaders for). Minimally opinionated

plugins.name: data-conventions
plugins.description: The team's opinionated house style for data code: uv, layout, ocha-lens first, geo stack, marimo idioms. Advisory; switchable per repo

plugins.name: aa-methods
plugins.description: AA methodology: trigger-design (vocabulary, spec→analysis→report, mandatory historical validation) + return-periods (Weibull, individual/overall/effective, all-in vs split)

plugins.name: infra-ops
plugins.description: Operating deployed pipelines: Databricks two-axis dev/prod model, DAB conventions, dsci secrets, pipeline-registry-first debugging
